---
title: "LRPITT21: Ability-adjusted ITT effect of group assignment on receptive vocabulary (ROWPVT) (R)"

date: last-modified
date-format: "YYYY-MM-DD HH:mm:ss Z"

format:
  html:
    code-fold: true
    theme: cosmo
    number-sections: true
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-links:
      - repo
---

::: {.callout-warning title="Warning: Preliminary model output"}

This model output is preliminary and likely to change as the model evolves and further data are received.

If the model was not fitted in "reporting" mode, the results will be more approximate than where additional sampling has been performed.

:::

```{python}
# | echo: false
import json
from pathlib import Path

import arviz as az
import pandas as pd

_here = Path(".")
with open(_here / "config.json") as f:
    config = json.load(f)
trace = az.from_netcdf(_here / "trace.nc")
tau_summary = pd.read_csv(_here / "tau_summary.csv")
```

## Overview

LRPITT21 is the **general-ability-adjusted** ITT analysis for receptive vocabulary (ROWPVT) (`rowpvt`) -
the ability-robustness companion to LRPITT05, part of the LRPITT17-24 ability-adjusted
family. It uses the uniform LRPITT spec plus a linear adjustment for **baseline
nonverbal cognitive ability** - block design (`blocks`), recorded at t1 only. The
estimand is unchanged: the total effect of randomised assignment $G$ on
$R_{\text{post}}$ over the RCT phase.

::: {.callout-note title="Why adjust for ability, and what it does (and does not) buy"}

Block design is a child trait measured **before** randomisation, so - like SES - it
**cannot confound** the randomised effect: randomisation balances it across arms in
expectation. This adjustment is a **precision / chance-imbalance robustness check**,
not confounding control. The immediate-intervention arm started about **0.27 SD
higher** in block design (a mild chance imbalance), and block design is prognostic of
the outcomes (most strongly for vocabulary), so adjusting for it asks whether the
effect survives that head-start.

Age is held in (`use_age_linear`), so the block-design term reads as ability **for
age** - the general latent ability construct. Block design is **complete for all 54
children**, so no rows are dropped: LRPITT21 vs LRPITT05 is a same-sample
adjusted-vs-unadjusted contrast and **no matched comparator is needed**.

:::

::: {.callout-note title="Identification and sign convention"}

Under the locked causal DAG, $G$ is a randomised root, so the ITT effect $\tau$ is
identified by the **empty adjustment set**. The own baseline, linear age, and the
block-design adjuster are **precision terms only** - they sharpen $\tau$ but are not
what licenses the causal interpretation. **Sign convention:** $G = 1$ is the
intervention arm, so a **positive** $\tau$ means the intervention raises the outcome.

:::

## Model

$$
\eta_i = \alpha + \tau\, G_i
       + \gamma_{\text{own}}\, \text{logit}_*(R_{\mathrm{pre},i}, N)
       + \gamma_A\, A_{\text{std},i}
       + \gamma_{\text{blocks}}\, z(\text{blocks}_i),
$$

with Beta-Binomial likelihood $R_{\text{post},i} \sim \text{BetaBinomial}(N, \mu_i\kappa, (1-\mu_i)\kappa)$,
$\mu_i = \sigma(\eta_i)$, $N = 170$. Priors follow the shared template in `priors.py`.
The block-design adjuster enters as a linear $\gamma_{\text{blocks}}$ term (the same
weak $\text{Normal}(0, 0.3)$ prior as cross-baseline couplings); HSGPs are off by
default.

![Model diagram](model_graph.svg){#fig-model .lightbox fig-align="left"}

## Shared priors

![alpha](prior_alpha.svg){width=32%}
![tau](prior_tau.svg){width=32%}
![gamma_own](prior_gamma_own.svg){width=32%}
![gamma_age](prior_gamma_age.svg){width=32%}
![kappa](prior_kappa.svg){width=32%}

## Sampling diagnostics

```{python}
# | echo: false
diag = pd.read_csv(_here / "diagnostics.csv", index_col=0)
diag
```

![Trace](trace_plot.png){.lightbox}

![Energy](energy_plot.png){.lightbox}

![Posterior](posterior_plot.png){.lightbox}

## Posterior predictive check

![Posterior predictive](posterior_predictive_check.png){.lightbox}

## Treatment-effect summary

```{python}
# | echo: false
tau_summary
```

- `tau_logit_*`: posterior mean and equal-tailed central interval (coverage `ci_prob`) of $\tau$ on the logit scale.
- `tau_prob_*`: average marginal effect on the probability scale (risk difference), averaged over the fitted sample per posterior draw.
- `prob_tau_pos`: $P(\tau > 0) = P(\text{intervention helps})$.

Compare $\tau$ here against the unadjusted LRPITT05: a stable estimate means the
mild baseline-ability imbalance does not explain the effect.

## LOO-PSIS

```{python}
# | echo: false
# | output: asis
print("```\n" + open(_here / "loo.txt").read() + "\n```")
```

## Configuration

```{python}
# | echo: false
config
```
